steps.title: Identify the key contrast
steps.content: The passage contrasts “less obtrusive” seasonal signs on open land with “just as perceptible” changes marked by “less trite and familiar” cues. This tells us “obtrusive” refers to how obvious or noticeable something is.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
“Conspicuous” means standing out or being easily noticed—a direct fit for how “obtrusive” functions in the passage, where visible seasonal signs contrast with subtle ones. The other three don't relate to noticeability or prominence.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
